summary: China Mobile's new companion robot Lingxi can recognize and follow its owner.

summary: The majority of cellular IoT data traffic will come from the automotive sector, with key use cases including infotainment services such as video and audio streaming, as well as firmware over-the-air updates. #pressrelease

title: T-Mobile's new JVs fixate on fiber
summary: Marking a $2.7 billion investment that will aid a broader convergence strategy, T-Mobile has formed JVs with Oak Hill Capital and Wren House to acquire GoNetspeed, Greenlight Networks and i3 Broadband.

summary: T-Mobile has formed strategic fiber joint ventures with Oak Hill Capital and Wren House. #pressrelease

title: T-Mobile and Starlink pitch businesses on blended broadband
summary: The 5G-plus-satellite 'SuperBroadband' bundle from T-Mobile and Starlink promises coverage in every US ZIP code. There's a 36-month commitment attached to that simplicity.

summary: Roku said it now offers the Peacock Premium Plus tier in the US via The Roku Channel, its platform for subscriptions and free, ad-supported streaming services. #pressrelease

summary: Iridium CEO talks up L-band value given recent M&A activity for spectrum but says focus is on organic growth and complementing bigger D2D players.

title: Analysts weigh what's next for cable after 'Bloody Friday'

title: Verizon surpasses 6M FWA subs as priority shifts to fiber

summary: Across nearly 1.4 million survey responses, one variable predicts wireless market share better than brand, price or network quality: whether the carrier owns the fiber under the street.

title: Telin and DITO join forces to bolster cross-border connectivity
summary: The two companies agreed to strengthen regional connectivity between the Philippines and Indonesia, underscoring their shared commitment to expanding their networks and improving their services.

title: Household gigabit subscribers to reach 60% by 2030 – Omdia
summary: Residential households subscribing to gigabit broadband will increase from about one-third in 2025 to 60% in 2030, according to Omdia analysts, putting pressure on ISPs to deliver higher speeds without compromising revenue.
